Cape Angler

  • Reading
  • Deals

Cape Town · Western Cape

Tie stronger knots.
Fish better spots.

A focused reference for recreational anglers on the Cape. The essential knots, the spots worth driving to, and the gear worth buying — without the clutter.

Start with knotsExplore spots

01 — Essentials

Five knots that cover 95% of days on the water.

Tap a knot to see the step-by-step and a demo video. Learn these cold and you’ll lose fewer fish, faster.

02 — On the water

Spots worth the drive.

A curated map of shore, rock, harbour and estuary marks around the Cape Peninsula and False Bay. Click any spot below for directions.

  • Kalk Bay Harbour

    Harbour & pier

    Working fishing harbour on the False Bay coast. Pier and harbour wall access, good for hottentot, galjoen and the occasional yellowtail. Family-friendly and easy shore access.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    A recreational saltwater fishing permit (post office) is required.
    Directions

  • Miller's Point

    Rock & surf

    Classic Cape rock-angling venue south of Simon's Town. Deep water close in — targets include kob, yellowtail and geelbek. Watch the swell; not a spot for novices on a pushing tide.

    Skill level:
    Experienced anglers
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required. Falls within the Table Mountain National Park MPA — check current zoning.
    Directions

  • Simon's Town Jetty

    Jetty

    Sheltered jetty fishing inside Simon's Town naval harbour area. Hottentot, steentjies and panga from the wall. Good winter option when the rocks are blown out.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Directions

  • Strand Beach

    Surf

    Long sandy surf beach on the eastern side of False Bay. Kob, elf (shad) and white steenbras are the main targets. Best fished early morning or late afternoon on a pushing tide.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required. Observe seasonal closures for shad (elf).
    Directions

  • Muizenberg Beach

    Surf

    Gentle, wide beach — ideal for beginners learning to cast a surf rod. Shad, kob, and occasional galjoen on a running tide. Shark spotters operate here; heed the flags.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Directions

  • Cape Point

    Rock angling

    Serious ledge fishing at the tip of the Cape Peninsula. Big yellowtail and kob are possible but the approach is long, the ledges exposed, and the swell unforgiving. For experienced anglers only.

    Skill level:
    Experienced anglers
    Permit:
    Park conservation fee plus recreational saltwater permit. Much of the area is within the MPA — check zoning before fishing.
    Directions

  • Hout Bay Harbour

    Harbour

    Harbour wall and breakwater access on the Atlantic side. Hottentot, mackerel and the occasional snoek from the wall during winter runs.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Directions

  • Milnerton Lagoon

    Estuary

    Diep River mouth and lagoon system. Light tackle for white steenbras, leervis (garrick) and grunter. Best on an incoming tide, working lures or live prawn.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required. Check current local regulations on bait collection.
    Directions

  • Kommetjie — Long Beach

    Surf & rock

    Long sandy stretch running up toward Noordhoek, with rocky headlands at the Kom itself. Kob and shad on the beach, hottentot and galjoen from the rocks. Exposed to Atlantic swell.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Directions

  • Gordon's Bay Harbour Wall

    Harbour

    Sheltered harbour wall fishing at the eastern end of False Bay. Hottentot, steentjies and panga are the regulars. Good winter spot when the south-easter is blowing.

    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Directions

03 — Useful reading

Know before you cast.

Five articles on permits, species, tides and catch-and-release — the background that turns a day out into a catch.

  • Department of Forestry, Fisheries & the Environment

    Recreational Fishing Permits — South Africa

    What permits you need to fish recreationally in South Africa, where to get them, and the species limits that apply.

    6 min read · opens on dffe.gov.za

  • South African Association for Marine Biological Research

    Common Shore Species of the Western Cape

    Identification guide and size-limit reference for the fish you're most likely to land from Cape shorelines.

    10 min read · opens on saambr.org.za

  • SA Navy Hydrographic Office

    Reading Tides — When to Fish the Cape Coast

    Official tide tables for Cape Town and surrounding ports, plus guidance on which phase of the tide fishes best for common species.

    5 min read · opens on sanho.co.za

  • ORI — Oceanographic Research Institute

    Catch-and-Release Best Practices

    How to handle, photograph and release fish so they actually survive — critical reading if you tag-and-release.

    7 min read · opens on saambr.org.za

  • SA Fishing Magazine

    A Seasonal Fishing Calendar for the Cape

    Month-by-month rundown of what's running where in the Western Cape — from winter snoek to summer yellowtail.

    8 min read · opens on safishingmag.co.za

A reference site for recreational anglers on the Cape. No accounts. No tracking pixels beyond what Vercel ships.
